_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Surgical Correction of Tetralogy of Fallot Repair (Category - III Congenital Heart Disease)
clinical_indications: Stable, minimally cyanosed: Total repair at 6–12 months of age or earlier according to the institutional policy (Class I)

Symptomatic children of <6 months of age with significant cyanosis or history of spells despite therapy: Palliation (by systemic-to-pulmonary artery shunt or stenting of the ductus arteriosus/right ventricular outflow tract or pulmonary valve balloon valvuloplasty) or total repair depending on anatomy and center's experience (Class I)
Lower threshold for earlier surgery if no requirement of transannular patch is anticipated
Patients having TOF with absent pulmonary valve who are stable: Medical management till 1 year of age followed by total correction with repair of pulmonary artery branch dilation/aneurysm (Class I)

Patients with anomalous left anterior descending artery from the right coronary artery crossing the right ventricular outflow tract, who are likely to need RV-to-pulmonary artery conduit (Class I): <10 kg weight with significant cyanosis: Aortopulmonary shunt

Patients with anomalous left anterior descending artery from the right coronary artery crossing the right ventricular outflow tract, who are likely to need RV-to-pulmonary artery conduit (Class I): >10 kg weight: Total repair using conduit, or double-barreled approach after 2 years of age, when the child weighs >10 kg.

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure
Was the Echo/ Doppler report suggestive of Tetralogy of Fallot Repair?

cpd_claim.q: Are the indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show repair of the defect?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Does the detailed discharge summary mention the need for reoperation?

common_queries: Echo/Doppler report is not suggestive of Tetralogy of Fallot Repair
Clinical notes missing, incomplete, or do not clearly indicate procedure
Procedure / Operative notes not submitted or are incomplete
Post procedure ECHO stills do not show repair of the defect
Detailed Discharge Summary is missing or does not mention follow-up date
Detailed discharge summary mentions the need for reoperation

clinical_key_pointers: TOF is the most common cyanotic congenital heart disease with a prevalence of 0.4/1000 live births constituting about 5% of all congenital heart defects. The clinical signs and symptoms seen in infants generally vary in accordance with the degree of right ventricular outflow tract obstruction.

Almost two-thirds of newborns with TOF are acyanotic at birth, but by 6 months of age, over half of them have cyanosis at rest. This occurs because of worsening infundibular stenosis which increases right-to-left shunting across the VSD.

Intermittent hypercyanotic spells are one of the defining features of TOF. Peak incidence of these episodes occurs between the 2nd and 6th months of life, and these become infrequent after 2 years of age.
Patients with untreated TOF have an estimated 1-year, 3-year, and 10-year survival of 66%, 49%, and 24%, respectively.
surgical_options: Palliation (by systemic-to-pulmonary artery shunt or stenting of the ductus arteriosus/right ventricular outflow tract or pulmonary valve balloon valvuloplasty) or total repair
Total correction with repair of pulmonary artery branch dilation/aneurysm
Total repair using conduit, or double-barreled approach
